other_observers: None; I am the original finder and identifier.
specific_location: Brush Hollow Reservoir SWA. East shore, less than 100 meters north of the boat ramp, and about 50 meters east of the shoreline.
description: Found three small finches together. Obvious red frontal-crown patch. Dark lores and chin. Sides had thick brownish streaks; back also streak brown. One prominent white wing bar with a less obvious white bar above it. Very short, stout, yellow bill. Fairly average-length tail, slightly on the short side. Two males were present and had a reddish wash across the breast. I only managed a single photo of a single bird's rump, and it looks surprisingly unstreaked, but nothing in any of my other photos suggests Hoary Redpoll to me. I will include the rump photo simply for completeness.Behaviors: Primarily foraging on sunflowers near lake shore. Calling to one another occasionally, and making short flights within sunflowers. After watching and photographing for several minutes, I moved forward a few steps. Two of the birds flew out of sight, and the third flew high into a nearby tree, at which point I decided to leave them alone.Call: Occasionally gave thin rising call note.Plumage: Basic
photographer: Photographer: Tyler Stuart, Tyler Stuart, Tyler Stuart, Tyler Stuart, Tyler Stuart
